name: aesfix
title: aesfix
summary: tool for correcting bit errors in an AES key schedule
description:
tool for correcting bit errors in an AES key schedule
This program illustrates a technique for correcting bit errors in an AES
key schedule. It should be used with the output of the aeskeyfind program.
.
It is limited to AES-128 key schedules, and it can only correct unidirectional
1->0 bit errors.  For the most part it has been optimized for readability
rather than performance.
.
This package is useful to several activities, as forensics investigations.

commands.name: aesfix
commands.description: Correct bit errors in an AES key schedules

arguments.description: Corrects bit errors in an AES key schedule read from the specified hex-encoded file.
arguments.raw: -FILE  Corrects bit errors in an AES key schedule read from the specified hex-encoded file.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use aesfix because tool for correcting bit errors in an AES key schedule.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use aesfix as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `aesfix SCHEDULE-FILE` as documented upstream for aesfix.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair aesfix with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `aesfix --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.description: Corrects bit errors in an AES key schedule read from the specified hex-encoded file.
